channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Candidates for California Governor Debate: 5 Takeaways
item.description: The nonpartisan primary remains volatile after the departure of Eric Swalwell. A televised debate featured six leading candidates, but produced few fireworks.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:description: Candidates in California’s race for governor (from left): Matt Mahan, Xavier Becerra, Chad Bianco, Steve Hilton, Tom Steyer and Katie Porter.

item.title: Senate Plunges Into Vote Marathon as G.O.P. Advances ICE Funding Boost
item.description: Republicans pressed to pass a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for immigration enforcement. But first, in an overnight session, they beat back Democratic proposals aimed at lowering costs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Senate in an overnight session took steps toward adopting a budget plan that would lead to a $70 billion increase in immigration enforcement.

item.title: Combative, Defensive and Occasionally Contrite, Kennedy Walks a Fine Line
item.description: In four days of congressional testimony, the health secretary sought to please the White House and his MAHA base at the same time.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. congressional appearances came at a time of great flux for his department, including the nomination of Dr. Erica Schwartz, whose supportive views on vaccines diverge sharply from Mr. Kennedy’s, to lead the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

item.title: U.S. Settles Carter Page Wiretap Lawsuit for $1.25 Million
item.description: F.B.I. applications to surveil Mr. Page, a 2016 Trump campaign adviser, during the investigation into Russia’s interference in the election had myriad errors and omissions, an inspector general found.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods
item.media:description: While Carter Page’s wiretapping was a minor part of the Russia investigation, it took on major political significance.

item.title: How ‘Yes’ Won a Narrow Victory in Virginia’s Redistricting Battle
item.description: Northern Virginia carried the measure to victory even as turnout in Democratic areas lagged and nearly all of the state shifted right from last year’s governor’s race.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang, Luke Vrotsos and Caroline Soler
item.media:description: Virginia voters approved a redistricting ballot measure in a special election that was held on Tuesday.

item.title: Kalshi Fines and Suspends 3 Political Candidates for Betting on Their Races
item.description: The prediction market said the candidates violated new rules. The platform and its competitors face growing scrutiny from lawmakers and regulators over how political betting is policed.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy, Campbell Robertson and Corina Knoll
item.media:description: Kalshi users can wager on everything from how long the government shutdown will last to what show will top Netflix’s streaming numbers in a given week.

item.title: Man Who Stole Kristi Noem’s Purse Gets 3 Years in Prison
item.description: Ms. Noem, the homeland security secretary at the time, was at a restaurant in Washington with her family when her bag was taken with $3,000 and her security badge inside.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:description: Kristi Noem was eating with her family last April at the Capital Burger in downtown Washington when she was robbed.

item.title: King Charles Will Not Meet Epstein Victims During U.S. State Visit
item.description: Representative Ro Khanna had asked Buckingham Palace for a private meeting between the victims and King Charles during his planned visit to the U.S. next week.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Lawyers representing King Charles said he will not meet with victims of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse when the monarch visits the U.S. next week.

item.title: Man Is Sentenced to 20 Years in 1983 Killing of Oregon Woman
item.description: Teresa Peroni, 27, was last seen on July 4, 1983, in Selma, Ore. Marcus Sanfratello, the man seen with her that day, pleaded guilty to first-degree manslaughter after his arrest last year.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

item.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
item.description: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of the Navy John Phelan championed a major investment in new ships including a “Trump-class” battleship, but his superiors were dissatisfied with his handling of the initiative.

item.title: Not a Deal-Breaker: White House Downplays Iranian Action Near the Strait
item.description: President Trump’s threats have given way (for the moment, at least) to a more conciliatory tone about Iran’s actions in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump at the White House on Tuesday.

item.title: RFK Jr. Defends Trump’s Mathematically Impossible Drug Discount Claims
item.description: President Trump has claimed that he has secured discounts of 400 to 1,500 percent on prescription drugs. A price discount cannot be more than 100 percent because that would lower the price to zero.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

item.title: How Kevin Warsh Is Distancing Himself From Trump
item.description: Lawmakers questioned the ability of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick for Fed chair, to lead the central bank independently. Colby Smith explains how Mr. Warsh tried to distance himself from the president.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith, Sutton Raphael, Christina Djossa, Nikolay Nikolov and Jon Miller

item.title: Hundreds of Mothballs Spread on Florida Beach, Wildlife Officials Say
item.description: The mothballs, which are toxic to wildlife, appeared on St. Pete Beach this week, according to the state’s Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission. Officials are investigating the episode.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.media:credit: Universal Images Group Editorial, via Getty
item.media:description: A state wildlife officer told The Tampa Bay Times that he thought the mothballs had been spread on the beach to keep black skimmers, a protected bird species, from nesting there.

item.title: Redistricting Fight Turns to Florida and the Courtroom for Frustrated Republicans
item.description: A victory for Democrats in Virginia has left Republicans grumbling about their strategy and looking to the next phase of a coast-to-coast battle.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk, Nick Corasaniti, Reid J. Epstein and Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Octavio Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Florida State Capitol in Tallahassee.

item.title: Sean Plankey, Trump’s Pick to Lead Cybersecurity Agency, Withdraws His Nomination
item.description: Confirmation for the nominee, Sean Plankey, had been pending in the Senate for over a year.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sean Plankey speaking during a Homeland Security hearing in Washington last year.

item.title: Why Virginia’s New Map Matters for Democrats in the Midterms, and What’s Next
item.description: Attention now turns to Florida, and the Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:description: People at a watch party for the Virginia redistricting referendum on Tuesday night in Arlington.

item.title: F.B.I. Said to Have Investigated Times Reporter After Article on Patel’s Girlfriend
item.description: The bureau said it is not pursuing a case, but the scrutiny is an example of the Trump administration weighing whether to criminalize routine news gathering.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt
item.media:description: Kash Patel, the F.B.I. director, during a House hearing last month. A New York Times reporter wrote about how he used bureau personnel to provide his girlfriend with government security and transportation.

item.title: RFK Jr. Says His Department Advises All Children to Get Measles Vaccine
item.description: Testifying on Capitol Hill, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continued to back away from his criticism of the measles shot. But he spoke on behalf of his department, not himself.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, at the Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: Wildfires in Georgia Destroy Homes and Set Off Evacuations
item.description: The fires are also causing poor air quality, expected to last into at least Thursday, for a corner of the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff, Aimee Ortiz and Hannah Ziegler

item.title: Man Accused of Killing 3 in Spree Near Atlanta Dies in Jail
item.description: Olaolukitan Adon Abel, a 26-year-old Navy veteran, had been charged with killing a man and two women in a series of attacks across suburban Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.media:description: Flowers near the site where Lauren Bullis was killed outside Atlanta last week.

item.title: 2 Dead and More Injured After ‘Chemical Release’ at West Virginia Plant
item.description: As many as 30 others were treated at a hospital and at the scene after a “chemical release” of hydrogen sulfide at a plant in Nitro, W.Va., the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser and Mark Walker
item.media:description: Emergency workers outside the Catalyst Refiners plant in Nitro, W.Va., on Wednesday.

item.title: How the Southern Poverty Law Center Drew the Ire of Conservatives
item.description: Before the Justice Department filed charges against it, the group had faced scandal and critiques from both the left and the right.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, left, the acting attorney general, and Kash Patel, the F.B.I. director, at a news conference on Tuesday announcing the indictment against the Southern Poverty Law Center.

item.title: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
item.description: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Loading bombs onto a U.S. Air Force B-1 bomber at an air base in Britain last month.

item.title: As Democrats Outraise Republican Candidates, the G.O.P. Has This $600 Million Edge
item.description: Even as the party faces a worrisome political environment and its candidates are outraised by Democratic ones, powerful Republican groups and super PACs have built a colossal advantage.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

item.title: David Scott, Longtime Georgia Congressman, Dies at 80
item.description: A former Georgia state lawmaker, he supported Black communities in the House and drew attention for his refusal to retire despite health problems.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Representative David Scott at the Georgia Capitol in Atlanta in 2024.

item.title: Bessent Backs Financial Support for Oil-Rich U.A.E.
item.description: The Treasury secretary said that currency swap line would benefit both the United Arab Emirates and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent testified on Capitol Hill on Wednesday.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Oil Company Argument in Fight Over Great Lakes Pipeline
item.description: The justices sided with Michigan officials, who have raised environmental alarms and pushed to decommission an aging section of the pipeline.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Cydni Elledge for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Enbridge Energy pump station yard in Mackinaw City, Mich., in 2024. The aging section of pipeline at issue is part of a vast network of lines that carry crude oil and natural gas liquids through Wisconsin and Michigan, before terminating in Ontario.

item.title: C.D.C. Cancels Publication of Study Showing Benefits of Covid Vaccines
item.description: The report found that the shots cut the likelihood of hospitalizations, but the agency’s acting head said it gave an inaccurate picture of the vaccines’ effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Health Department official said Dr. Jay Bhattacharya had met with the study’s authors but that they had not wanted to change its design.

item.title: Supreme Court Finds Soldier Injured in Suicide Bombing Can Sue
item.description: An American soldier was injured in a 2016 suicide bombing in Afghanistan. The justices cleared the way for the soldier to proceed with a lawsuit against a military contractor.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Afghan soldiers outside Bagram Airfield in November 2016.

item.title: With Midterms Coming Up, G.O.P. Is Bogged Down in Congress
item.description: Republican majorities are at odds on big issues as the Senate resorts to extraordinary measures to pass homeland security funding over a Democratic blockade.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:description: The Capitol this week.

item.title: California’s Governor Candidates to Meet in First Major Debate After Swalwell
item.description: Six candidates for governor will face off Wednesday night in a race that has been upended by Eric Swalwell’s sudden departure.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Clockwise from top left: Jae C. Hong/Associated Press; Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times; Godofredo A. Vásquez/Associated Press (2); Jae C. Hong/Associated Press; Godofredo A. Vásquez/Associated Press
item.media:description: Clockwise from top left: Xavier Becerra, Katie Porter, Steve Hilton, Tom Steyer, Chad Bianco, and Matt Mahan.

item.title: F.A.A. Investigates Near Miss Between Passenger Jets at J.F.K. Airport
item.description: An American Airlines regional jet flew close to an Air Canada regional jet after aborting its landing. It was the second such incident in recent days at a major U.S. airport.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: The F.A.A. said it was investigating a close call between two planes at Kennedy International Airport.

item.title: College Where Charlie Kirk Was Killed Revokes Graduation Speaker’s Invite
item.description: Utah Valley University was thrilled that Sharon McMahon, a best-selling author, would speak at its graduation. And then her old posts resurfaced.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy W. Peters
item.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Author Sharon McMahon had spoken at Utah Valley twice before.

item.title: California Is Rife With Hospice Fraud. But Whose Fault Is It?
item.description: The Trump administration and Democratic state leaders are blaming each other for what everyone agrees is a big problem. The president has targeted California and Gov. Gavin Newsom in particular.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, the former talk show host who now runs the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, has been an outspoken emissary of the Trump administration’s campaign against California.

item.title: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott Uses Trump-Style Tactics in Fight With Cities Over ICE
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott had given the leaders of Houston, Dallas and Austin until Wednesday to amend their policies to his liking, or return over $150 million in police funding.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Greg Abbott of Texas intensified his rhetoric on Monday on social media.

item.title: A $440,000 Breast Reduction: How Doctors Cashed In on the No Surprises Act and Arbitration
item.description: A law meant to end surprise medical billing accidentally created a multibillion-dollar industry that is making doctors richer.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff and Margot Sanger-Katz
item.media:credit: Andres Kudacki for The New York Times

item.title: In Virginia Redistricting Win, Democrats Play Hardball to Thwart Trump
item.description: “We cannot bring a stick to a knife fight”: Democrats are increasingly open to extreme measures, including gerrymandering, when the stakes are high.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:description: Signs in support of the Virginia redistricting referendum at a polling location at Nottingham Elementary School in Arlington, Va., on Tuesday.

item.title: A Capitol Police Officer, Bogus Accusations and a Jan. 6 Conspiracy Theory
item.description: Shauni Kerkhoff was wrongfully implicated in the notorious Capitol Hill pipe-bomb case. Can she ever fully move on?
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll
item.media:credit: Alyssa Schukar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shauni Kerkhoff and her fiancé, Daniel Dickert, served as Capitol Police officers during the insurrection on Jan. 6, 2021.
